insights.summary: Plaud AI maintained momentum from CES 2026 coverage but missed key opportunities to capitalize on Humane AI's negative cycle. The 'AI Note Taker' label persists across 62% of coverage, indicating insufficient narrative evolution toward the target 'AI Work Companion' positioning.
insights: Long-tail CES pickup concentrated in tech trade publications rather than mainstream business media, limiting enterprise audience reach
Humane AI's product failures created a 72-hour window to position Plaud as 'the AI wearable that actually works' - opportunity missed
Bloomberg and CNBC remain unengaged despite Otter.ai securing enterprise-focused coverage in both outlets this week
Current messaging emphasizes capture functionality over the full Extract-Utilize workflow, reinforcing note-taking perception
actions: Pitch Bloomberg Technology on 'AI Work Companion vs AI Note Taker' angle with enterprise adoption data by Thursday
Secure CNBC Squawk Box segment contrasting Plaud's practical AI approach with Humane's failed promises
Brief top 3 spokespeople to lead with 'work companion' language in all interviews, retire 'note taker' entirely
Launch reactive campaign highlighting Plaud's reliability amid AI wearable skepticism using #AIThatWorks hashtag
Coordinate customer case studies showcasing Extract-Utilize workflow for Friday enterprise media push
insights.risk: Continued positioning as 'AI Note Taker' commoditizes Plaud against established players like Otter.ai while missing the higher-value work companion category.
insights.opportunity: Humane AI's stumbles created market skepticism that Plaud can uniquely address by demonstrating practical, reliable AI wearable execution.
